cards.name: ESL Conversation Club at the Cupertino Library
cards.blurb: Drop into the ESL Conversation Club at Cupertino Library — even if English is your first language, it's a genuinely warm, interesting group of people to spend a morning with.
cards.why: Free, low-key, and a surprisingly good way to spend a weekday morning.

cards.name: Rancho San Antonio County Park & Open Space Preserve
cards.address: Cupertino, CA, USA
cards.blurb: Rancho San Antonio is the real deal for a midday hike — take the lower loop past the farm, or push up to Regnart Creek trail if you want to actually break a sweat.
cards.why: The clouds should clear by noon and the hills will look great after the cool morning.

cards.name: Rio Adobe Southwest Cafe
cards.address: 10525 S De Anza Blvd #100, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: Rio Adobe is a solid post-hike lunch stop — get the tacos or a burrito bowl and eat outside if it warms up enough.
cards.why: Under $15 and genuinely good Southwest-style food, which is exactly what you want after a trail.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: The State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU is one of those shows that messes with your perception in the best way — give yourself time to actually sit with each piece instead of rushing through.
cards.why: It's free and a great way to spend the afternoon without fighting any weather.

cards.name: MeloMelo Coconut Dessert
cards.address: 19652 Stevens Creek Blvd, Cupertino, CA 95014, USA
cards.blurb: MeloMelo in Cupertino does coconut desserts done right — get a bowl with the classic toppings and take your time with it as a proper evening wind-down.
cards.why: It's a perfect low-key ending to the day and the kind of spot you'll want to come back to.

cards.name: Los Gatos Cafe Uptown
cards.address: 15662 Los Gatos Blvd, Los Gatos, CA 95032, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning at this Los Gatos cafe with a proper breakfast and good coffee before the day gets going.
cards.why: Solid local spot that closes at 2 PM, so morning is the move.

cards.name: Triton Museum of Art
cards.address: 1505 Warburton Ave, Santa Clara, CA 95050, USA
cards.blurb: Walk through the galleries and take your time in the sculpture garden — it's free, uncrowded, and genuinely impressive for a local museum.
cards.why: Free contemporary art with a sculpture garden is a perfect way to spend a slow Sunday morning.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Check out this optical illusion and perception-based exhibition — the kind of show that's actually fun to walk through, not just stare at.
cards.why: It's free, it's in Santa Clara, and it pairs perfectly with the Triton visit for an art-heavy afternoon.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Pick a vendor or three and graze your way through lunch at San Pedro Square — tacos, banh mi, ramen, coffee, whatever sounds good.
cards.why: Best all-in-one food stop in the area and a great spot to settle in and decompress for a bit.

cards.name: Slingshot Pinball
cards.address: 460 Lincoln Ave #70, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Grab a machine at Slingshot and play pinball for a couple hours — they've got a great selection and it's genuinely fun for adults.
cards.why: Beats sitting around and it's one of the better ways to spend a rainy Sunday evening.

cards.why: It's creative, relaxed, and a solid way to decompress after a full day out.

cards.name: State of Illusion Exhibition
cards.blurb: Wander through the State of Illusion Exhibition at SCU — the kind of visual art show that messes with your perception in the best way.
cards.why: It's free and a genuinely interesting way to spend a cool morning.

cards.name: Fists of Fury at King Library
cards.blurb: Head over to King Library and check out Fists of Fury, an exhibition digging into the history and culture of martial arts in America.
cards.why: Free, fascinating, and the kind of thing you'd never stumble on otherwise.

cards.name: San Pedro Square Market
cards.address: 87 N San Pedro St, San Jose, CA 95110, USA
cards.blurb: Grab lunch at San Pedro Square Market and mix and match from a dozen vendors — tacos, ramen, banh mi, whatever sounds good. Snag a patio seat and take your time.
cards.why: It's the most fun way to do lunch downtown when you can't agree on one thing.

cards.name: SJSU Student Union Bowling Center
cards.address: 211 S 9th St, San Jose, CA 95192, USA
cards.blurb: Bowl a few frames at the SJSU Student Union Bowling Center. It's a real lanes setup, not a pop-up, and it's open to the public.
cards.why: Low-key competitive and way more fun than it sounds on a Monday afternoon.

cards.name: Santana Row
cards.address: 377 Santana Row, San Jose, CA 95128, USA
cards.blurb: Stroll Santana Row in the early evening, browse the boutiques, and find a table at one of the outdoor restaurants when you're ready to sit down.
cards.why: The boulevard is relaxed on a weekday evening and the patio vibes are solid.

cards.name: LUNA Mexican Kitchen - San Jose
cards.address: 1495 The Alameda, San Jose, CA 95126, USA
cards.blurb: Wrap the night with dinner at LUNA Mexican Kitchen on The Alameda. Order margaritas and something off the dinner menu and call it a good Monday.
cards.why: Solid Mexican food with a vibe that doesn't feel rushed.

cards.blurb: Drop in on the South Bay Needlepointers Stitch-In, a relaxed community gathering where people bring their needlepoint projects and hang out. You don't have to be an expert — just show up, see what people are working on, and enjoy the chill vibe.
cards.why: It's free, it's low-key, and it's a genuinely nice way to spend a Thursday morning.

cards.name: Sandy Wool Lake - Gazebo
cards.address: 901 Downing Rd, Milpitas, CA 95035, USA
cards.blurb: Head over to Sandy Wool Lake Gazebo for a mid-morning walk around the lake before lunch. It's a peaceful spot, great for clearing your head and getting some fresh air while the temperature climbs toward that 71°F high.
cards.why: Perfect weather for a lakeside stroll before settling in for lunch.

cards.blurb: Grab lunch at Sugar Mama Desserts in the Ranch 99 Shopping Center. The dessert-forward menu has something for every sweet tooth, and it makes for a fun, indulgent lunch stop.

cards.name: Mountain View Farmers Market
cards.address: 600 W Evelyn Ave, Mountain View
cards.blurb: Wander the stalls at the Mountain View Farmers Market, pick up fresh fruit, maybe a pastry, and ease into the morning without any agenda.
cards.why: Thursday morning, cool air, and a farmers market is the right way to start a day off.

cards.name: Computer History Museum
cards.address: 1401 N Shoreline Blvd, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Give yourself a solid two hours at the Computer History Museum. The Revolution exhibit alone covers room-sized computers through the smartphone era and the displays are genuinely wild, not dry.
cards.why: Even if you think you're not a tech person, this place pulls you in.

cards.name: Dish N Dash
cards.address: 736 N Mathilda Ave, Sunnyvale, CA 94085, USA
cards.blurb: Head to Dish N Dash for lunch. Get the shawarma or a mezze plate and sit down properly rather than grabbing something fast.
cards.why: It's some of the best Middle Eastern food in the South Bay and won't wreck your wallet.

cards.name: ESL Conversation Club
cards.blurb: Drop into the ESL Conversation Club in Los Altos. It's a laid-back group conversation session, great for meeting people and just talking.
cards.why: Free, social, and a totally different vibe from anything else in the day.

cards.name: Artisan Wine & Spirits
cards.address: 2495 Old Middlefield Way, Mountain View, CA 94043, USA
cards.blurb: Stop into Artisan Wine and Spirits on Old Middlefield for a proper browse. Grab a bottle of something interesting for the evening or just taste around if they're pouring.
cards.why: It's a genuinely good shop with a well-curated selection and a relaxed feel.

cards.name: Next Level Board Game Cafe
cards.address: 888 Villa St, Mountain View, CA 94041, USA
cards.blurb: Finish the night at Next Level Board Game Cafe. Order food and drinks, pick a game from their library, and settle in for the evening.
cards.why: There is no better Thursday night move than a board game cafe with good company.

cards.name: Yummy Future Coffee, Matcha & Tea
cards.address: 170 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Start the morning with a matcha latte or a creative tea drink at this University Ave spot — good energy, good vibes before the day gets going.
cards.why: Best way to ease into a cool April morning.

cards.name: The Dish Trail
cards.address: Stanford Foothills (Stanford Ave trailhead)
cards.blurb: Hike the Dish loop up through the Stanford foothills — the views of the Bay stretch out wide from the top and the climb is just hard enough to feel earned.
cards.why: High of 70°F means it'll be perfect up there before it warms up.

cards.name: Tamarine Restaurant & Gallery
cards.address: 546 University Ave, Palo Alto, CA 94301, USA
cards.blurb: Lunch at Tamarine on University Ave. The Vietnamese food here is genuinely elevated, so go for the shaken beef or whatever the lunch special is and take your time.
cards.why: It's a legitimately great restaurant and lunch is the smart move for the price.

cards.name: Cantor Arts Center
cards.address: Cantor Arts Center, Stanford University
cards.blurb: Walk through the Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus and spend time in the Rodin Sculpture Garden outside — the bronze figures are massive and worth slowing down for.
cards.why: Free world-class art and the campus makes for a great afternoon stroll.

cards.name: Volunteering at Stanford Educational Farm - Weekday
cards.address: 175 Electioneer Rd, Stanford, CA 94305
cards.blurb: Swing by the Stanford Educational Farm and volunteer for an hour or so — weeding, planting, whatever they've got going that afternoon.
cards.why: It's a genuinely fun way to wrap up an afternoon outdoors.

cards.name: Dinah's Poolside Restaurant
cards.address: 4261 El Camino Real, Palo Alto, CA 94306, USA
cards.blurb: Dinner at Dinah's Poolside Restaurant. Sit on the patio if it's warm enough, order something classic off the menu, and enjoy a proper sit-down meal to end the day.
cards.why: The poolside setting hits different on a clear spring evening.

Tuesday in Palo Alto, fully planned and ready to go 🌅

Start your morning right: Matcha latte or creative tea at Yummy Future Coffee on University Ave — the kind of opening that sets the tone for a good day.

Then lace up and head to the Dish Trail. The loop through the Stanford foothills is classic for a reason — the views of the Bay open up wide from the ridge, and you'll remember why you live here.

By noon, you'll be ready for lunch. Tamarine on University Ave does Vietnamese food that actually feels elevated. Go hungry.

After lunch, take the walk to Cantor Arts Center on Stanford's campus. Spend some quiet time in the Rodin Sculpture Garden — it's the kind of pause a good Tuesday needs.

Mid-afternoon, swing by the Stanford Educational Farm and volunteer for an hour. Weeding, planting, whatever they need — it's grounding work that actually feels useful.

Finish with dinner at Dinah's Poolside. If it's warm, sit on the patio and order something classic. Watch the day wind down in the best way.
